body:
Hi Juan,

Thanks for reaching out. I looked at your December statement — the extra $18 is a one-time device protection add-on that was applied in error. I've removed it and credited your account.

Denise, Consumer Cellular Support

That explains it. Thank you for catching that and issuing the credit.

Juan

Hi,

Just following up — the dropped calls are still happening, mostly in the back of the house. Restarting the phone didn't help.

Juan

Hi Juan,

Thanks for the detail. There's maintenance on a nearby tower expected to finish next week, which should improve things. In the meantime, turning on Wi-Fi Calling on your iPhone will let calls run over your home internet. I can send step-by-step instructions if that helps.

Marcus, Consumer Cellular Support

Please do send the Wi-Fi Calling steps. I'll try that while the tower work wraps up.

Juan

Hi Juan,

Happy to help — I've switched your account back to mailed paper statements and kept the emailed copy active as well. The first paper bill will arrive with next month's cycle.

Priya, Consumer Cellular Support

Perfect, that's exactly what I wanted. Thank you.

Juan

Hi Juan,

I'm sorry about the outage. I do see an area event affecting West Annapolis this morning; crews are already on it. I've also sent a refresh signal to your gateway. Please leave it powered on and it should reconnect within the hour.

Tanya, Xfinity Support

The light turned white and I'm back online. Thanks for the quick help.

Juan

Hi Juan,

Thanks for the report. Evening slowdowns are often local congestion or an aging gateway. Could you run a speed test tonight around the time it slows and reply with the numbers? That will help me pinpoint whether it's your line or the equipment.

Andre, Xfinity Support

Ran it last night at 8pm — only 42 Mbps down. Midday it was over 380. I'll keep an eye on it.

Juan

That confirms an evening congestion issue on your node. Engineering has been notified and is balancing the load; you should see improvement over the next few days. I'll keep this ticket open until it's confirmed resolved.

Andre, Xfinity Support

Hi Juan,

Sorry the gateway is acting up. You're due for an upgrade anyway, so I've shipped a new unit at no charge — it should arrive in 3 to 5 business days with a prepaid return label for the old one. Setup is plug-and-play, but call us if you'd like to be walked through it.

Rachel, Xfinity Support

The new gateway arrived and set up on the first try. I'll drop the old one at the UPS store. Thank you.

Juan

Hi Juan,

Comcast email needs a separate app password when you add it to the Mail app on your iMac, which is why the regular password is rejected. Sign in to your account online, generate an app password, and use that in Mail with server imap.comcast.net (port 993). I've emailed the full steps as well.

Tanya, Xfinity Support

The app password did it — mail is syncing on the iMac now. Much appreciated.

Juan

Hi Juan,

Thanks for flagging this quickly. The autopay declined because the card's expiration date on file had rolled over. I've updated the expiration, taken this month's payment, and confirmed there's no late fee or interruption to your service.

Denise, Consumer Cellular Support

Thank you for handling it right away and waiving the fee. Glad it's sorted.

Juan

Hi Juan,

Thanks for reaching out. I looked at your December statement — the extra $18 is a one-time device protection add-on that was applied in error. I've removed it and credited your account.
